steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The three notes report three different outcomes — positive, neutral, and negative effects. Any valid generalization must account for this full pattern of variation, not cherry-pick one result or claim consistency.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Seed ingestion by bats can have varying effects on seed germination.": Directly reflects the three distinct outcomes shown across the studies, accurately synthesizing all the data. ✓
  • "Over the years, researchers have studied the effect that seed germination has had on ingestion by bats.": Flips the direction of the relationship; the studies measure effects in one direction only. ✗
  • "As was found in the 2017 study, ingestion by bats has a negative effect on the germination of plant seeds.": Contradicts the actual findings reported in the notes. ✗
  • "Bat ingestion has consistently been found to have a positive effect on seed germination.": Claims uniformity when one study found neutral and another found negative effects. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
